# `draw.fillBetween`

> **Stability:** stable
> **Since:** 0.4
> **Bucket:** `polylines`
> **Wire kind:** `fill-between`

Fill the ribbon between two edges. The native equivalent of Pine
`linefill.new(line1, line2, color)` and `fill(plot1, plot2, color)`.
The filled region is the closed polygon `edgeA` forward then `edgeB`
reversed; the two edges need not share x-coordinates or length.

## Anchors

`edgeA`, `edgeB` — two `WorldPoint` lists (the band edges)

Anchor count: variable — each edge needs 2..10000 finite anchors.

## Signature

```ts
function fillBetween(edgeA: ReadonlyArray<WorldPoint>, edgeB: ReadonlyArray<WorldPoint>, opts?: FillBetweenStyle): DrawingHandle;
```

_The leading `slotId: string` parameter is injected by the chartlang compiler at every callsite — script authors call `draw.fillBetween(...)` without it._

## Example

```ts
import { type WorldPoint, defineIndicator } from "@invinite-org/chartlang-core";
    const top: WorldPoint[] = [];
    const bottom: WorldPoint[] = [];
    export default defineIndicator({
        name: "draw.fillBetween demo",
        apiVersion: 1,
        compute({ bar, draw }) {
            top.push({ time: bar.time, price: bar.high });
            bottom.push({ time: bar.time, price: bar.low });
            if (top.length >= 2) {
                draw.fillBetween(top, bottom, { fill: "#3b82f6", fillAlpha: 0.2 });
            }
        },
    });
```
